introduction.p: Choose the correct inequality or equal sign to make the relation true.

statement.p: Let x and y be integers such that x \lt y\text{.} Then x - y y - x\text{.}

solution.p: Since x \lt y\text{,} this means that x-y results in a negative number and y-x results in a positive number. Thus, x-y < y-x\text{.}

statement.p: Let x and y be integers, such that 1 \lt x \lt y\text{.} Then xy x + y\text{.}

solution.p: This problem comes down to looking at the impact of multiplying by y compared to the impact of adding y\text{.} Since x\gt1\text{,} x is at least 2\text{.} Similarly, y is at least 3\text{.} Multiplying by y results in a bigger number than adding y\text{.}

statement.p: Let x and y be fractions such that 0 \lt x \lt y \lt 1\text{.} Then xy x + y\text{.}

solution.p: This problem comes down to looking at the impact of multiplying by y compared to the impact of adding y\text{.} Since 0 \lt y \lt 1\text{,} this means that y is a fraction between 0 and 1\text{.} Thus, multiplying by y results in a smaller number, whereas adding y results in a bigger number.

statement.p: Let x and y be integers, such that x \lt y\text{.} Then x + 2y 2x + y\text{.}

solution.p: Notice that x + 2y and 2x + y both have an x and a y\text{.} Lets subtract an x and a y from those expressions.
Since y \gt x\text{,} this means that x + 2y \gt 2x + y\text{.}
